reaches.name: Redwood Creek Reach A (within 200 yards of the mouth)
Redwood Creek Reach B (200 yards above the mouth to the mouth of Prairie Creek)
reaches.toDesc: Mouth of Prairie Creek (about 1 mile upstream of Orick)

reaches.name: Redwood Creek Reach C (mouth of Prairie Creek to the mouth of Bond Creek)
reaches.toDesc: Mouth of Bond Creek (near Tall Trees Grove, Redwood National Park)

reaches.name: Redwood Creek Reach D and tributaries, above the mouth of Bond Creek

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40(b)(77) — Redwood Creek (Humboldt Co.)
sources.quotedText: (77) Redwood Creek (Humboldt Co.). Also see Section 8.00(a) Low-Flow Restrictions. (A) Redwood Creek main stem, within a radius of 200 yards of its mouth. Jan. 1 through Mar. 31. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession. (B) Redwood Creek main stem, from 200 yards above the mouth to the mouth of Prairie Creek. Fourth Sat. in May through Mar. 31. Only barbless hooks may be used. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession. (C) Redwood Creek main stem, from the mouth of Prairie Creek to the mouth of Bond Creek. Fourth Sat. in May through Mar. 31. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession. (D) Redwood Creek and tributaries, above the mouth of Bond Creek. Closed to all fishing all year.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — footnote definition of hatchery trout/steelhead (**)
sources.quotedText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released. Wild trout or steelhead are those not showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is present).

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(117) — Redwood Creek (Humboldt Co.)
sources.quotedText: (117) Redwood Creek (Humboldt Co.) and tributaries above the mouth of Bond Creek. Closed to all fishing all year.

sources.title: CCR T14 §8.00(a) — Low-Flow Restrictions on Angling
2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (CDFW booklet)
Redwood Creek — Fishing the North Coast with Kenny Priest
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

regulations.humanSummary: Open January 1 through March 31 within a 200-yard radius of the mouth.
regulations.verbatimText: Redwood Creek main stem, within a radius of 200 yards of its mouth. Jan. 1 through Mar. 31.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used, within 200 yards of the mouth.
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: Applies only to hatchery (adipose-fin-clipped) trout or steelhead; combined daily bag of 2, combined possession limit of 4.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily bag; 4 in possession, within 200 yards of the mouth.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: Open the fourth Saturday in May through March 31, from 200 yards above the mouth to the mouth of Prairie Creek.
regulations.verbatimText: Redwood Creek main stem, from 200 yards above the mouth to the mouth of Prairie Creek. Fourth Sat. in May through Mar. 31.

parameters.note: Barbless hooks required, but bait is allowed (unlike Reaches A and C, which restrict tackle to artificial lures only).

regulations.humanSummary: Only barbless hooks may be used (bait permitted), from 200 yards above the mouth to the mouth of Prairie Creek.
regulations.verbatimText: Only barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: Applies only to hatchery (adipose-fin-clipped) trout or steelhead; combined daily bag of 2, combined possession limit of 4.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily bag; 4 in possession, from 200 yards above the mouth to the mouth of Prairie Creek.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: Open the fourth Saturday in May through March 31, from the mouth of Prairie Creek to the mouth of Bond Creek.
regulations.verbatimText: Redwood Creek main stem, from the mouth of Prairie Creek to the mouth of Bond Creek. Fourth Sat. in May through Mar. 31.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used, from the mouth of Prairie Creek to the mouth of Bond Creek.
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: Applies only to hatchery (adipose-fin-clipped) trout or steelhead; combined daily bag of 2, combined possession limit of 4.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily bag; 4 in possession, from the mouth of Prairie Creek to the mouth of Bond Creek.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.note: Redwood Creek and all tributaries upstream of (above) the mouth of Bond Creek, within Redwood National Park, are closed to all fishing year-round to protect headwater rearing and spawning habitat for steelhead, coastal cutthroat trout, and salmon.

regulations.humanSummary: Redwood Creek and tributaries above the mouth of Bond Creek are closed to all fishing all year.
regulations.verbatimText: Redwood Creek and tributaries, above the mouth of Bond Creek. Closed to all fishing all year.

parameters.note: Only hatchery trout or steelhead showing a healed adipose fin clip may be retained in Reaches A, B, and C; wild (unclipped) trout or steelhead must be released immediately.

regulations.humanSummary: Wild (unclipped) trout and steelhead must be released immediately; only adipose-fin-clipped hatchery fish count toward the bag.
regulations.verbatimText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released. Wild trout or steelhead are those not showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is present).

parameters.description: Also see 14 CCR §8.00(a) — Low-Flow Restrictions, which may impose additional emergency closures on this water when streamflow drops below regulatory thresholds.

regulations.humanSummary: Low-flow restrictions under §8.00(a) may additionally close or restrict fishing on Redwood Creek during periods of low streamflow.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
